name: vscode-copilot-custom-provider
description: A configurable VS Code language model provider backed by an OpenAI Responses API compatible endpoint.

commands.title: Custom OpenAI Responses: Set API Key
Custom OpenAI Responses: Clear API Key

copilotCustomProvider.enabled.description: Enable the custom language model provider.

copilotCustomProvider.profiles.markdownDescription: Base URL/key groups exposed under this provider. Each profile has its own `baseUrl`, API key stored under the profile id in VS Code SecretStorage, headers, request body overrides, and model list. After configuring profiles, run `Custom OpenAI Responses: Set API Key` for each profile that requires a key.

id.markdownDescription: Stable profile id. Used to build the default VS Code model id as `profileId/modelId`.

name.description: Human-readable profile name shown in model details and API key commands.

baseUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL for this profile, for example `https://host-a.example.com`, `https://host-a.example.com/v1`, or `https://host-a.example.com/proxy`. URLs without an explicit API path get `/v1/responses` appended, matching VS Code Custom Endpoint URL resolution for `responses`.

apiKey.description: API key for this profile. Prefer the Set API Key command, which stores the key in VS Code SecretStorage.

requireApiKey.description: Require an API key when this profile is used. Models still appear in the picker so users can configure the key later.

apiKeyHeader.description: Optional profile-level API key header override. When omitted, the provider follows VS Code Custom Endpoint behavior: `api-key` for URLs containing `openai.azure`, otherwise `Authorization`.

apiKeyPrefix.markdownDescription: Prefix prepended when `apiKeyHeader` is explicitly set. Use `Bearer ` for bearer headers, or an empty string for raw key headers.

extraHeaders.description: Additional HTTP headers sent for this profile. Values are sent as strings. Reserved, unsafe, and auth-related header overrides are ignored.

requestBodyOverrides.description: Additional JSON body fields merged into every request for this profile.

models.markdownDescription: Models exposed for this profile. `id` is the model id exposed under the profile, while `apiModel` is the upstream Responses API model value. The VS Code/Copilot id is `profileId/modelId`, so the same `apiModel` can be exposed more than once by giving each entry a different `id`.

id.markdownDescription: Model id exposed under this profile. The VS Code/Copilot id is `profileId/modelId`. Sent to the Responses API only when `apiModel` is omitted.

apiModel.description: Actual model value sent to the Responses API. Defaults to id.

name.markdownDescription: Base display name for this model. The final picker name is produced by `copilotCustomProvider.modelNameTemplate`, where this value is `${modelName}`.

baseUrl.markdownDescription: Optional base URL or full Responses API URL override for this model. It uses the same automatic `/v1/responses` rule as the profile `baseUrl`.

family.description: Model family identifier exposed to VS Code.

version.description: Model version exposed to VS Code.

maxInputTokens.description: Maximum input token budget advertised to VS Code.

maxOutputTokens.description: Maximum output token budget advertised to VS Code and sent as max_output_tokens.

toolCalling.description: Advertise tool calling support and forward VS Code tools to the Responses API. A number advertises the maximum number of tools accepted.

thinking.markdownDescription: Advertise thinking/reasoning metadata support for this model. When `true`, Responses requests include `reasoning.encrypted_content`, encrypted reasoning items are round-tripped, and `temperature` is removed from the final request body.

streaming.markdownDescription: Whether this model supports streaming. Set `false` to send `stream: false` even when global streaming is enabled.

editTools.markdownDescription: Edit tool preference hints exposed through VS Code's language model capabilities, aligned with VS Code BYOK `editTools`.

reasoningEffort.markdownDescription: Fallback reasoning effort injected into Responses API requests for this model. The value is not restricted by this schema; use one of this model's advertised `supportsReasoningEffort` levels. When `supportsReasoningEffort` is omitted or `[]`, the advertised levels default to `minimal`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, and `xhigh`.

supportsReasoningEffort.markdownDescription: Reasoning effort levels the model accepts, for example `["minimal", "low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"]`. Because this provider is Responses-only, omitting this property or setting it to `[]` uses the provider default five levels. Set a non-empty array to use those exact picker values. The provider contributes a `configurationSchema.properties.reasoningEffort` entry so VS Code/Copilot can show the native picker and pass the selected value as `options.modelConfiguration.reasoningEffort`.

temperature.description: Optional temperature sent to the Responses API request. Removed for thinking models.

topP.description: Optional top_p sent to the Responses API request.

zeroDataRetentionEnabled.markdownDescription: Whether Zero Data Retention (ZDR) is enabled for this endpoint. When `true`, `previous_response_id` will not be sent and requests use `store: false`.

supportedEndpoints.markdownDescription: Responses endpoints supported by this model, aligned with VS Code Custom Endpoint metadata. Include `ws:/responses` to send requests over Responses WebSocket v2; otherwise the extension uses HTTP/SSE `/responses`.

requestHeaders.markdownDescription: Model-level request headers, aligned with VS Code Custom Endpoint `requestHeaders`. `authorization` and `api-key` can override the default auth header; `${apiKey}` is replaced with the profile API key.

extraBody.description: Additional JSON body fields merged into requests for one model.

patch.markdownDescription: Small compatibility patches applied after all request body fields are merged. Defaults are conservative and do not change request semantics unless explicitly enabled.

drop.markdownDescription: Compatibility switches that delete selected fields from the outgoing Responses API request.

truncation.markdownDescription: Delete the top-level `truncation` field from the outgoing Responses API request. Some third-party relay APIs cannot correctly handle Copilot's `truncation: "disabled"` field; enable this only when debugging shows removing that field fixes the relay. Default `false` preserves normal request semantics.

copilotCustomProvider.defaultReasoningEf.description: Reasoning effort used when a model does not set reasoningEffort. It is applied only if the value is included in that model's advertised supportsReasoningEffort levels, which default to the provider five levels.

copilotCustomProvider.enableStreaming.description: Request streaming responses from the target Responses API URL.

copilotCustomProvider.maxRetries.description: Number of retries for failed non-cancelled HTTP requests.

copilotCustomProvider.tokenEstimateChars.description: Approximate characters per token used by provideTokenCount.

copilotCustomProvider.modelNameTemplate.markdownDescription: Template for model names shown in the Copilot/Chat model picker. Useful because all models share the `Custom OpenAI Responses` provider group. Supported placeholders: `${profileId}`, `${profileName}`, `${modelId}`, `${modelName}`, `${apiModel}`, `${reasoningEffort}`, `${baseUrlHost}`.

copilotCustomProvider.logLevel.markdownDescription: Logging level for the extension output channel. `off` disables request logs, `info` logs request metadata, and `debug` logs the outgoing HTTP request including redacted headers and JSON body. Debug logs can contain prompt content.

copilotCustomProvider.logRequests.markdownDescription: Legacy request metadata logging switch. Prefer `copilotCustomProvider.logLevel`; when `logLevel` is not configured, `true` is treated as `logLevel: "info"`.

copilotCustomProvider.requestBodyOverrid.description: Additional JSON body fields merged into every Responses API request.

scripts.package: npm run clean && npm run compile && vsce package --no-dependencies
